steps.title: Identify what the assertion requires
steps.content: Rodriguez and colleagues assert that four of the five exoplanets are hot Jupiters (mass > 0.25 Jupiters, period < 10 days) and one is a warm Jupiter (mass > 0.25 Jupiters, period > 10 days). Supporting data must show all five meeting the mass threshold and exactly one planet with a period exceeding 10 days.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'None of the planets have an orbital period of more than 10 days, and TOI-628 b has a mass of 6.33 Jupiters.': The table shows TOI-1478 b has a period of 10.180 days, which is more than 10 days — so this claim is false. ✗
  • Choice B — 'TOI-1478 b has an orbital period of 153 days, and the masses of all the planets range from 0.85 to 6.33 Jupiters.': TOI-1478 b's period is 10.180 days, not 153 days — the figure is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'All the planets have a radius between 1.060 and 1.771 Jupiters, and only TOI-1333 b has an orbital period of more than 10 days.': TOI-1333 b has a period of 4.720 days (less than 10), not more than 10; and radius is irrelevant to the hot/warm Jupiter classification. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Each of the planets has a mass greater than 0.25 Jupiters, and all except for TOI-1478 b have an orbital period of less than 10 days.': All five masses (0.85–6.33 Jupiters) exceed 0.25, and TOI-1478 b is the sole planet with a period above 10 days (10.180). This perfectly supports four hot Jupiters and one warm Jupiter. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
